metadata.note: Visual scaffolding drawn from staged research synthesis and staged source/indicator registers. It contains qualitative watch states, missing-value markers, and source IDs, not national scores or reviewed indicator values.

layers.label: Frontier Access
layers.description: The level of AI capability actors can build, buy, import, deploy, or adapt.
layers.interface_note: Shown as access conditions and constraints, not as a national AI rank.

layers.label: Conversion Capacity
layers.description: Complementary assets that turn AI into repeated operational use in firms, public services, labs, infrastructure, and defense.
layers.interface_note: Includes compute, energy, industrial data, systems integrators, procurement, and organizational redesign.

layers.label: Adaptation Capacity
layers.description: Whether institutions, labor markets, governance, and infrastructure can absorb disruption.
layers.interface_note: Kept separate from adoption so exposure is not mistaken for social adjustment.

layers.label: Distribution Quality
layers.description: Whether gains diffuse broadly or concentrate among elite firms, frontier labs, state actors, or favored regions.
layers.interface_note: Shown through gaps, pass-through questions, and missingness rather than a headline score.

layers.label: Realized Outcomes
layers.description: Observed changes in productivity, industrial capability, science, public services, military capacity, welfare, and adjustment costs.
layers.interface_note: V0 treats this as the hardest layer; most values remain missing until source review.

sources.title_english: Stanford AI Index / Global AI Vibrancy Tool
sources.limitations: Version and methodology changes require careful year handling.

sources.title_english: Eurostat enterprise AI and ICT use tables
sources.limitations: Definitions vary over time and are EU-focused.

sources.authors_org: Erik Brynjolfsson; Daniel Rock; Chad Syverson

countries.name: United States
countries.summary: Frontier-heavy system with strong labs, capital, and suppliers; V0 conversion questions focus on grid absorption, permitting, public procurement, and long-tail enterprise diffusion.

layer_states.note: Staged research points to strong frontier inputs. No country score is assigned.
Local power, interconnection, and large-load queues are treated as deployment constraints to test.
Relevant sources are staged, but no reviewed adaptation value is available.

layer_states.status: firm-size and regional gaps to measure
layer_states.note: Distribution must be measured directly; national adoption is not broad pass-through.

layer_states.status: case evidence exists; national outcomes missing
layer_states.note: Task- or case-level evidence cannot be promoted to national conversion outcomes.

countries.name: China
countries.summary: State-coordinated deployment and manufacturing depth are central hypotheses; V0 keeps chip constraints, official target validation, labor absorption, and outcome evidence separate.

layer_states.note: Chip and accelerator access are tested as constraints, not treated as settled values.
Official policy and target evidence must be separated from independent outcome validation.

layer_states.status: labor absorption and governance stress open
layer_states.note: No reviewed country-level adaptation value is available in the canonical data layer.
Official platform scale signals are not treated as broad welfare pass-through.
AI+ and smart-factory targets are not realized outcomes without independent validation.

countries.name: Japan
countries.summary: Comparator for robotics depth, factory know-how, and workforce aging; most V0 values are missing until Japanese sources are reviewed.

layer_states.note: Official strategy is staged but not a frontier-access value.
The hypothesis is useful for the sector module, but no V0 country score is assigned.
Displayed as a watch state, not as an adaptation score.
No reviewed distribution-quality value is available.
No reviewed AI outcome value is available.

countries.name: South Korea
countries.summary: Comparator for automation intensity and SME smart-manufacturing policy; outcome claims remain staged and official.

layer_states.note: Strategy architecture is not frontier capability itself.
Program claims require outcome validation and comparable maturity definitions.
The watch state points to evidence needs, not a measured value.
SME reach should be measured directly before claims are made.
No reviewed cross-country outcome value is available.

countries.name: Germany
countries.summary: Comparator for industrial standards, automotive data spaces, and energy constraints; staged sources do not yet support a country score.

layer_states.note: No reviewed frontier-access value is available.
Industrial interoperability and data-center strategy are staged as conversion factors.
Adaptation claims need reviewed labor and training data.
Firm-size diffusion gaps must remain separate from national adoption.
No reviewed AI-attributed outcome value is available.

countries.name: Singapore
countries.summary: Comparator for small-state public-sector and enterprise diffusion; land, power, and scale limitations must travel with any claim.

layer_states.note: Official strategy is not a measured frontier-access value.
Small-country structure limits comparability with large industrial systems.
Displayed as official policy evidence, not outcome performance.
No reviewed broad pass-through value is available.
No reviewed AI-attributed outcome value is available.

countries.name: India
countries.summary: Comparator for public compute, digital public infrastructure, and inclusion potential; firm-level conversion and outcome evidence remain sparse.

layer_states.note: Official GPU and compute claims need validation before becoming access values.
Public infrastructure reach is an enabling channel, not an AI outcome.
Exposure, skills, and safety-net variables need direct values.
Digital reach does not establish broad AI gains without pass-through evidence.
No reviewed AI-attributed outcome value is available.

axes.label: Chip and Frontier Access
axes.description: Advanced accelerators, packaging, frontier labs, and model access.

axes.label: Energized Power
axes.description: Local data-center power, interconnection, firm supply, water, cooling, and latency fit.

axes.label: Enterprise Absorption
axes.description: Workflow redesign, management depth, data maturity, and pilot-to-scale speed.

axes.label: Industrial Interoperability
axes.description: Sensors, industrial data, standards, OT cybersecurity, integrators, and maintenance networks.

axes.label: Labor and Legitimacy
axes.description: Training, social license, regulation, remedies, and labor-market adjustment.

axes.label: Broad Pass-Through
axes.description: SME, regional, worker, patient, citizen, and public-service diffusion.

DPI reach is not broad AI welfare evidence without pass-through indicators.

factors.label: Robot Density and Stock
factors.description: Industrial robot density and operational stock. Candidate source exists, but V0 country values are missing.

factors.label: Annual Robot Installations
factors.description: Automation investment flow. Values are not loaded into V0.

factors.label: Manufacturing AI Adoption
factors.description: Share of manufacturing enterprises using AI where survey definitions permit.

factors.label: Smart-Factory Programs
factors.description: Program counts, maturity labels, and official targets kept separate from outcomes.

factors.label: Industrial Data and Standards
factors.description: Interoperability, standards participation, and manufacturing data spaces.

factors.label: Integrators and OT Support
factors.description: Systems integrators, maintenance technicians, and OT cybersecurity support.

factors.label: Operational Improvements
factors.description: Defect, downtime, OEE, cycle-time, or safety improvements with baseline and method.

options.label: US frontier lead
options.description: The United States reaches stronger frontier systems first.

options.label: Narrow capability gap
options.description: Frontier model gaps compress quickly across leading systems.

options.label: Open-model compression
options.description: Open or widely licensed models reduce the value of a closed frontier lead.

options.label: Energy and grid
options.description: Local power, interconnection, cooling, or permitting constrains deployment.

options.label: Chips and compute
options.description: Accelerator access and high-density compute availability bind.

options.label: Workflow depth
options.description: Firms and agencies cannot redesign workflows fast enough.

options.label: Market-led diffusion
options.description: Frontier firms, cloud providers, and large customers diffuse first.

options.label: State-coordinated AI+
options.description: Policy, public procurement, and sector programs steer deployment.

options.label: Standards-led industry
options.description: Interoperability, compliance, and industrial standards govern diffusion.

options.label: Broad pass-through tested
options.description: Worker, SME, regional, public-service, and welfare evidence is required.

options.label: Elite concentration risk
options.description: Gains accrue mainly to frontier firms, elite regions, or state actors.

options.label: Distribution missing
options.description: Distribution effects are unknown and should remain visibly unresolved.

cases.label: Frontier Lead, Bottlenecked Deployment
cases.description: A stronger frontier model does not become durable advantage if local power, permitting, public procurement, or enterprise absorption slows deployment.

signals.label: Frontier access strong; conversion bottleneck watch
signals.note: Test whether data-center and enterprise bottlenecks slow national conversion.

signals.label: Frontier access constrained; deployment machinery hypothesis
signals.note: Test whether AI+ and grid coordination offset weaker frontier access in specific domains.

cases.caveat: This is a hypothesis pattern, not a forecast.

cases.label: Good-Enough Systems, Faster Diffusion
cases.description: A narrower model gap makes workflow redesign, industrial data, and deployment coordination more important than frontier benchmarks.

signals.label: Manufacturing and AI+ conversion hypothesis
signals.note: Use official claims as leads to verify, not as outcomes.

signals.label: Long-tail diffusion and SME absorption question
signals.note: Adoption surveys need intensity and distribution breakouts.

cases.caveat: Treat China-US advantage as domain-specific and contingent.

cases.label: Standards-Led Industrial Conversion
cases.description: Industrial interoperability, data spaces, safety standards, and shop-floor know-how become the binding layer for embodied AI.

signals.label: Interoperability pathway to test
signals.note: Catena-X-style claims need independent and comparable outcome evidence.

signals.label: Robotics and shop-floor knowledge pathway
signals.note: Robotics depth does not automatically imply AI productivity gains.

cases.caveat: The simulator does not convert these pathways into rankings.

cases.label: Compute Sovereignty and Public Rails
cases.description: Public compute, sovereign AI programs, and digital public infrastructure increase access, but conversion depends on delivered capacity and broad pass-through.

signals.label: Public compute and DPI pathway
signals.note: Official program claims need delivered-capacity and usage validation.

cases.caveat: Public access rails are enabling infrastructure, not realized outcomes.

claim_cards.title: Frontier access is not the same as conversion.
claim_cards.claim: The Atlas should keep frontier capability, complementary deployment assets, adaptation, distribution, and outcomes in separate layers.
claim_cards.caveat: Theory supports the separation, but it does not provide country values.

claim_cards.title: Local power is a conversion constraint, not just an infrastructure footnote.
claim_cards.claim: AI deployment can fail at the local load pocket even when national electricity supply and capital availability look adequate.
claim_cards.caveat: National totals are too coarse; values must be region- and load-pocket-specific.

claim_cards.title: Manufacturing conversion needs a middle layer.
claim_cards.claim: Robot stock, industrial data, sensors, interoperability standards, systems integrators, maintenance, and worker training should be measured before claiming AI production outcomes.
claim_cards.caveat: Smart-factory labels and program counts are not comparable outcomes without validation.

claim_cards.title: Distribution quality cannot be inferred from adoption.
claim_cards.claim: AI adoption by firms or public agencies does not establish worker, SME, regional, citizen, or welfare pass-through.
claim_cards.caveat: Distribution claims need direct evidence on gaps, exposure, cushioning, and outcomes.

uncertainty_legend.label: observed
uncertainty_legend.definition: A candidate source describes directly observed data, but the visual still shows missing when no reviewed local value has been loaded.

uncertainty_legend.label: official-claim
uncertainty_legend.definition: A law, strategy, target, public program, or official statistic. Keep separate from independent validation.

uncertainty_legend.label: qualitative-coded
uncertainty_legend.definition: A staged research judgment or rubric slot that must remain visibly non-numeric.

uncertainty_legend.label: estimated
uncertainty_legend.definition: A modeled or inferred value. V0 uses this label only when a method is documented.

uncertainty_legend.label: missing
uncertainty_legend.definition: No reviewed value. Missingness is rendered directly rather than filled for symmetry.

uncertainty_legend.label: placeholder
uncertainty_legend.definition: Schema or example material that is not evidence.
